Final Project - Predicting PDAC with Circulating miRNA

2026-04-14

Preparing Data

Import Data

We use the normalized miRNA count matrix from GEO and merge it with the demographic metadata. Categorical variables are set to factors and the PDAC case and control variable is set to binary for modeling.

The dataset is fully observed with no missing values, so we can proceed without imputation.

Create Training and Holdout Sets

To split the data while respecting matched-pairs, we performed the split on the matched set IDs, ensuring each pair is not split between sets.

Training and holdout set sizes
dataset rows
Training 2090
Holdout 524
Training and holdout class balance
dataset class n_samples
Training Controls 1045
Training PDAC case 1045
Holdout Controls 262
Holdout PDAC case 262

Perform Differential Expression Filtering

The limma filtering is based on a blocked linear model with duplicate-correlation adjustment for matched sets and is run once on the full training set. In that model, CC is the term of interest and Sex, Race, and Age are adjustment covariates. The top N miRNAs are selected based on the smallest limma p-values, with ties broken by the absolute moderated t-statistic.

Top 50 miRNAs selected by the blocked limma filter on the full training set

The top-ranked miRNAs table has 50 rows and 10 columns. The selected miRNAs vector has 50 miRNAs. These top 50 miRNAs are then used as the shared candidate feature set for all models in the subsequent cross-validation step, with the rationale that this filtering step is necessary to reduce the feature space to a level that can be effectively handled by the models given the sample size, while still being broad enough to allow for model-specific feature selection and not biasing towards any one model’s selection criteria.

Cross-Validation Loop

We perform a 5-fold cross-validation on the training set of paired samples, ensuring matched-samples are kept together in the same fold.

Within each fold: 1. We restrict the normalized count matrix to the top 50 miRNAs selected from the limma analysis on the full training set. 2. We then fit each model using that shared subset of miRNAs together with Sex, Race, and Age in the design. The matched_set identifier is used for grouped splitting and blocked limma, but not as a predictive covariate because holdout matched sets are unseen by construction.

Cross-validation AUC and accuracy by model
model n_predictions cv_auc cv_accuracy
enet 2090 0.595 0.570
lasso 2090 0.593 0.568
forward_step 2090 0.578 0.551
lda 2090 0.575 0.554
random_forest 2090 0.559 0.533
decision_tree 2090 0.524 0.518

Holdout Evaluation

Evaluate the final fold, using the calibrated models from the CV step, on the holdout set. We report AUC and accuracy for each model on the holdout set, as well as the number of features selected in each final model and the number of miRNAs that passed the initial limma filter.
